data.description:
## Why
Code inspection suggests Jira link/relation sync is scaffolded (client primitives in acli_graph.py: set_relationship/get_issue_links/delete_issue_link) but NOT wired into the diff/fetch/apply pipeline (OutboundMutation.links always []; fetcher/inbound have no link refs; no link MutationAction; no caller of the primitives). The user expects links SHOULD sync. Settle it EMPIRICALLY with tests + a live probe before re-scoping story 25ae.

## What
- Integration tests (in-process, real pipeline) that drive a rebar ticket carrying a link (blocks/depends_on) through the OUTBOUND differ and assert whether a link mutation is produced; and feed a Jira snapshot containing issuelinks through the INBOUND differ and assert whether a relation change is produced.
- A live probe (gated, authorized LIVE DIG): create two Jira issues + a rebar link between their tickets, run reconcile DRY-RUN, and observe whether the dry-run PLAN contains a link mutation (non-destructive); clean up any created issues.
- Report RAW results (do not assume outcome): does outbound emit links? does inbound reflect links? does the live dry-run plan include link ops?

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] Integration tests exist exercising outbound + inbound link paths through the real differs
- [ ] A gated live probe runs reconcile dry-run with a real cross-issue rebar link and records whether links appear in the plan
- [ ] Empirical verdict recorded (works / partially / not wired) with evidence
- [ ] Suite green; ruff clean
data.title: Experimentally verify Jira link-sync status (integration tests + live probe)
